docs.title: ISO 29100 / Privacy Framework: LinkML Schema
docs.text:
A comprehensive LinkML schema modeling the ISO/IEC 29100:2024 privacy framework for information and communication technology (ICT) systems. Captures actors and roles, PII recognition, privacy safeguarding requirements, the eleven privacy principles, and the privacy risk management process. This schema covers: - Privacy framework actors (PII principal, controller, processor, third party) - Personally identifiable information (PII) and its categories - Privacy principles (11 principles from Clause 6) - Privacy safeguarding requirements and influencing factors - Privacy risk management including privacy impact assessment - Privacy policies, privacy controls, consent records, and privacy preferences

URI: https://w3id.org/lmodel/iso29100

Name: iso29100

docs.title: Enum: ConsentMechanism
docs.text:
The mechanism by which a PII principal provides or withholds consent for PII processing, as referenced in Clauses 3.6 and 6.2.

URI: iso29100:ConsentMechanism

docs.title: Permissible Values
docs.text: Value Meaning Description opt_in dpv:ExplicitlyExpressedConsent The PII principal must take an explicit action to express prior consent for t... opt_out dpv:ImpliedConsent The PII principal must take a separate action to withhold or withdraw consent... implied None Consent implied by some action of the PII principal, such as placing an order...

docs.title: LinkML Source
docs.text:
name: ConsentMechanism
annotations:
  iso29100_clause:
    tag: iso29100_clause
    value: '3.6'
description: The mechanism by which a PII principal provides or withholds consent
  for PII processing, as referenced in Clauses 3.6 and 6.2.
in_subset:
- privacy_framework_core
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/iso29100
rank: 1000
permissible_values:
  opt_in:
    text: opt_in
    description: The PII principal must take an explicit action to express prior consent
      for their PII to be processed for a particular purpose.
    meaning: dpv:ExplicitlyExpressedConsent
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '3.6'
  opt_out:
    text: opt_out
    description: The PII principal must take a separate action to withhold or withdraw
      consent; processing is presumed permitted unless that action is taken.
    meaning: dpv:ImpliedConsent
  implied:
    text: implied
    description: Consent implied by some action of the PII principal, such as placing
      an order or using a service, where applicable law permits.

docs.title: Class: ConsentRecord
docs.text:
Schema class documenting a PII principal's agreement to processing of their PII for stated purposes. Records the consent mechanism used, scope, current status, and withdrawal date — supporting auditability of the consent and choice privacy principle.

URI: iso29100:ConsentRecord

 classDiagram

    click ConsentRecord href "../ConsentRecord/"

      ConsentRecord : principal_ref

      ConsentRecord : withdrawal_date

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
• Consent must be freely given, specific, and informed
  • Opt-in required for sensitive PII unless applicable law permits otherwise
  • Withdrawal of consent must be easily exercisable and free of charge
  • Reference: ISO/IEC 29100:2024 Clause 3.4 and 6.2. ISO/IEC standards text is copyright ISO - not reproduced here.

docs.title: Class: NamedEntity
docs.text:
Abstract base class for all entities with a unique identifier, name, and description. Provides common identification and documentation slots.

  • NOTE: this is an abstract class and should not be instantiated directly

docs.title: Class: PIIController
docs.text:
Schema class for an organization or individual that determines why and how PII is processed. Captures appointed processors, legal bases relied upon, privacy policy reference, and data protection officer details. Bears ultimate accountability for privacy principle adherence.

URI: iso29100:PIIController

 classDiagram
    class PIIController
    click PIIController href "../PIIController/"
      PrivacyStakeholder <|-- PIIController
        click PrivacyStakeholder href "../PrivacyStakeholder/"

      PIIController : appointed_processors





        PIIController --> "*" PIIProcessor : appointed_processors
        click PIIProcessor href "../PIIProcessor/"



      PIIController : contact_information

      PIIController : created_date

      PIIController : data_protection_officer

      PIIController : description

      PIIController : id

      PIIController : jurisdictions

      PIIController : legal_bases

      PIIController : modified_date

      PIIController : name

      PIIController : privacy_policy_ref





        PIIController --> "0..1" PrivacyPolicy : privacy_policy_ref
        click PrivacyPolicy href "../PrivacyPolicy/"



      PIIController : processing_purposes

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
• May appoint PII processors to process PII on its behalf while retaining responsibility
  • More than one PII controller may exist for the same PII set
  • Reference: ISO/IEC 29100:2024 Clause 3.8 and 5.2.3. ISO/IEC standards text is copyright ISO - not reproduced here.

docs.title: Enum: PIIFlowRole
docs.text:
The role played by an actor in a specific PII flow scenario, as described in Table 1 of ISO/IEC 29100:2024 Clause 5.3.

URI: iso29100:PIIFlowRole

docs.title: Permissible Values
docs.text: Value Meaning Description pii_provider None Actor that provides PII to another party in this flow scenario pii_recipient None Actor that receives PII from another party in this flow scenario not_involved None Actor not directly involved in this specific PII flow scenario

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Name Description pii_principal_role Role of the PII principal in this flow scenario pii_controller_role Role of the PII controller in this flow scenario pii_processor_role Role of the PII processor in this flow scenario third_party_role Role of the third party in this flow scenario

docs.title: In Subsets
docs.text: • PrivacyFrameworkCore

docs.title: Annotations
docs.text: property value iso29100_clause 5.3

docs.title: Schema Source
docs.text: • from schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/iso29100

docs.title: Class: PIIPrincipal
docs.text:
Schema class for a data subject — the individual to whom PII belongs. Links consent records and privacy preferences to the natural person whose data is being processed, enabling subject-centric data models.

URI: iso29100:PIIPrincipal

 classDiagram
    class PIIPrincipal
    click PIIPrincipal href "../PIIPrincipal/"
      PrivacyStakeholder <|-- PIIPrincipal
        click PrivacyStakeholder href "../PrivacyStakeholder/"

      PIIPrincipal : consent_records





        PIIPrincipal --> "*" ConsentRecord : consent_records
        click ConsentRecord href "../ConsentRecord/"



      PIIPrincipal : contact_information

      PIIPrincipal : created_date

      PIIPrincipal : description

      PIIPrincipal : id

      PIIPrincipal : jurisdictions

      PIIPrincipal : modified_date

      PIIPrincipal : name

      PIIPrincipal : privacy_preferences





        PIIPrincipal --> "*" PrivacyPreference : privacy_preferences
        click PrivacyPreference href "../PrivacyPreference/"

docs.title: Class: PIIProcessingActivity
docs.text:
Schema class documenting a PII processing operation: its purpose, legal basis, operation types performed, categories of PII involved, responsible actors, applied controls, retention period, and any cross-border transfer safeguards.

docs.title: Enum: PIIProcessingOperation
docs.text:
Categories of operations that can be performed on PII, as referenced in the definition of processing of PII (Clause 3.21).

URI: iso29100:PIIProcessingOperation

docs.title: Permissible Values
docs.text: Value Meaning Description collection dpv:Collect Gathering or acquiring PII from PII principals or other sources storage dpv:Store Storing PII in a persistent manner in ICT systems or other media alteration dpv:Transform Modifying or updating existing PII retrieval dpv:Access Accessing or fetching stored PII for use consultation dpv:Use Reviewing or examining PII without necessarily extracting it disclosure dpv:Disclose Making PII available to other parties or making it accessible anonymization dpv:Anonymise Irreversibly altering PII such that the PII principal can no longer be identi... pseudonymization dpv:Pseudonymise Replacing identifying information in PII with an alias, retaining linkability... dissemination dpv:Disseminate Widely distributing or publishing PII to multiple recipients deletion dpv:Erase Removing PII such that it is no longer accessible or usable destruction dpv:Remove Permanently and irrecoverably eliminating PII and its carriers

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Name Description processing_operations Types of processing operations performed on this PII operations_performed Processing operations performed in this activity processing_restriction_preferences Specific processing operations the PII principal wishes to restrict

name: PIIProcessingOperation
annotations:
  iso29100_clause:
    tag: iso29100_clause
    value: '3.21'
description: Categories of operations that can be performed on PII, as referenced
  in the definition of processing of PII (Clause 3.21).
in_subset:
- pii_recognition
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/iso29100
rank: 1000
permissible_values:
  collection:
    text: collection
    description: Gathering or acquiring PII from PII principals or other sources.
    meaning: dpv:Collect
  storage:
    text: storage
    description: Storing PII in a persistent manner in ICT systems or other media.
    meaning: dpv:Store
  alteration:
    text: alteration
    description: Modifying or updating existing PII.
    meaning: dpv:Transform
  retrieval:
    text: retrieval
    description: Accessing or fetching stored PII for use.
    meaning: dpv:Access
  consultation:
    text: consultation
    description: Reviewing or examining PII without necessarily extracting it.
    meaning: dpv:Use
  disclosure:
    text: disclosure
    description: Making PII available to other parties or making it accessible.
    meaning: dpv:Disclose
  anonymization:
    text: anonymization
    description: Irreversibly altering PII such that the PII principal can no longer
      be identified directly or indirectly.
    meaning: dpv:Anonymise
  pseudonymization:
    text: pseudonymization
    description: Replacing identifying information in PII with an alias, retaining
      linkability while restricting direct identification.
    meaning: dpv:Pseudonymise
  dissemination:
    text: dissemination
    description: Widely distributing or publishing PII to multiple recipients.
    meaning: dpv:Disseminate
  deletion:
    text: deletion
    description: Removing PII such that it is no longer accessible or usable.
    meaning: dpv:Erase
  destruction:
    text: destruction
    description: Permanently and irrecoverably eliminating PII and its carriers.
    meaning: dpv:Remove

name: PIISensitivityCategory
annotations:
  iso29100_clause:
    tag: iso29100_clause
    value: '3.23'
description: Categories of PII based on sensitivity, distinguishing standard PII from
  sensitive PII as defined in Clause 3.23.
in_subset:
- pii_recognition
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/iso29100
rank: 1000
permissible_values:
  standard:
    text: standard
    description: PII that does not meet the threshold for sensitive PII but still
      requires appropriate privacy protection measures.
  sensitive:
    text: sensitive
    description: PII warranting heightened protection due to its potential for significant
      adverse impact on the individual, or because applicable law classifies it as
      a special category. Typical examples include health, biometric, financial, racial,
      religious, political, and sexual orientation data. Processing may require opt-in
      consent or specific legal authorization.
    meaning: dpv:SpecialCategoryPersonalData
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '3.23'

docs.title: Class: PrivacyBreach
docs.text:
Schema class for a recorded incident in which PII was processed in a manner that violates applicable privacy safeguarding requirements. Tracks breach details, affected PII categories, notification obligations, remediation actions, and lessons learned.

docs.title: Class: PrivacyControl
docs.text:
Schema class for a protective measure that manages privacy risks within the framework. Can be organizational, physical, technical (including Privacy Enhancing Technologies), or legal-contractual in nature; linked to the principles and safeguarding requirements it addresses.

URI: iso29100:PrivacyControl

docs.title: Enum: PrivacyControlType
docs.text:
Categories of privacy controls that treat privacy risks by reducing their likelihood or consequences, as noted in Clause 3.12 Note 1.

URI: iso29100:PrivacyControlType

  likelihood or consequences, as noted in Clause 3.12 Note 1.

    meaning: dpv:PhysicalMeasure

      privacy enhancing technologies (PETs).
    meaning: dpv:TechnicalMeasure
  legal_contractual:
    text: legal_contractual
    description: Legal contracts, binding corporate rules, or data processing agreements
      implementing privacy safeguards.
    meaning: dpv:LegalMeasure

docs.title: Subset: PrivacyFrameworkCore
docs.text:
Core privacy framework elements: actors, roles, PII concept, interactions, and the overall framework container (Clauses 5.1-5.3).

URI: PrivacyFrameworkCore

docs.title: Comments
docs.text: • Covers the fundamental building blocks of the privacy framework

docs.title: Class: PrivacyImpactAssessment
docs.text:
Schema class for a structured evaluation of the privacy implications of a processing activity or ICT system. Records assessment scope, identified and treated risks, accepted residual risks, stakeholder consultation records, and overall assessment outcome.

URI: iso29100:PrivacyImpactAssessment

    click PrivacyImpactAssessment href "../PrivacyImpactAssessment/"
      NamedEntity <|-- PrivacyImpactAssessment

      PrivacyImpactAssessment : assessment_outcome

      PrivacyImpactAssessment : assessment_scope

      PrivacyImpactAssessment : assessor

      PrivacyImpactAssessment : description

        click PIIProcessingActivity href "../PIIProcessingActivity/"

        PrivacyImpactAssessment --> "*" PrivacyRisk : residual_risks_accepted
        click PrivacyRisk href "../PrivacyRisk/"

        click PrivacyRisk href "../PrivacyRisk/"

docs.title: Class: PrivacyPolicy
docs.text:
Schema class for a governing document expressing an organization's intentions and commitments for PII processing. Covers both the internal policy document and any external privacy notice made available to PII principals (referred to as a notice in this framework).

URI: iso29100:PrivacyPolicy

 classDiagram
    class PrivacyPolicy
    click PrivacyPolicy href "../PrivacyPolicy/"
      NamedEntity <|-- PrivacyPolicy
        click NamedEntity href "../NamedEntity/"

      PrivacyPolicy : applicable_principles





        PrivacyPolicy --> "*" PrivacyPrinciple : applicable_principles
        click PrivacyPrinciple href "../PrivacyPrinciple/"



      PrivacyPolicy : contact_for_inquiries

      PrivacyPolicy : created_date

      PrivacyPolicy : data_retention_statement

      PrivacyPolicy : description

      PrivacyPolicy : effective_date

      PrivacyPolicy : id

      PrivacyPolicy : is_external_notice

      PrivacyPolicy : last_review_date

      PrivacyPolicy : modified_date

      PrivacyPolicy : name

      PrivacyPolicy : pii_categories_covered

      PrivacyPolicy : pii_principal_rights

      PrivacyPolicy : policy_scope

      PrivacyPolicy : policy_version

      PrivacyPolicy : processing_purposes_covered

docs.title: Class: PrivacyPreference
docs.text:
Schema class capturing a PII principal's processing choices for a particular purpose scope: preferences for anonymity or pseudonymity, restrictions on specific processing operations, and restrictions on disclosure to named or categorized parties.

URI: iso29100:PrivacyPreference

 classDiagram
    class PrivacyPreference
    click PrivacyPreference href "../PrivacyPreference/"
      NamedEntity <|-- PrivacyPreference
        click NamedEntity href "../NamedEntity/"

      PrivacyPreference : anonymity_preference

      PrivacyPreference : created_date

      PrivacyPreference : description

      PrivacyPreference : disclosure_restriction_preferences

      PrivacyPreference : id

      PrivacyPreference : modified_date

      PrivacyPreference : name

      PrivacyPreference : preference_recorded_date

      PrivacyPreference : preference_scope

      PrivacyPreference : principal_ref

      PrivacyPreference : processing_restriction_preferences





        PrivacyPreference --> "*" PIIProcessingOperation : processing_restriction_preferences
        click PIIProcessingOperation href "../PIIProcessingOperation/"



      PrivacyPreference : pseudonymity_preference

docs.title: Enum: PrivacyPrinciple
docs.text:
The eleven privacy principles defined in ISO/IEC 29100:2024 Clause 6 that govern the processing of PII in ICT systems.

URI: iso29100:PrivacyPrinciple

docs.title: Permissible Values
docs.text: Value Meaning Description consent_and_choice dpv:InformedConsent Schema value for privacy principle 1 (Clause 6 purpose_legitimacy_and_specification dpv:Purpose Schema value for privacy principle 2 (Clause 6 collection_limitation None Schema value for privacy principle 3 (Clause 6 data_minimization None Schema value for privacy principle 4 (Clause 6 use_retention_and_disclosure_limitation None Schema value for privacy principle 5 (Clause 6 accuracy_and_quality None Schema value for privacy principle 6 (Clause 6 openness_transparency_and_notice None Schema value for privacy principle 7 (Clause 6 individual_participation_and_access dpv:DataSubjectRight Schema value for privacy principle 8 (Clause 6 accountability None Schema value for privacy principle 9 (Clause 6 information_security None Schema value for privacy principle 10 (Clause 6 privacy_compliance dpv:ComplianceAssessment Schema value for privacy principle 11 (Clause 6

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Name Description applicable_principles Privacy principles this policy addresses addressed_principles Privacy principles this control helps implement principle The privacy principle being implemented

docs.title: In Subsets
docs.text: • PrivacyPrinciples

docs.title: Annotations
docs.text: property value iso29100_clause 6 principle_count 11

docs.title: Schema Source
docs.text: • from schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/iso29100

docs.title: LinkML Source
docs.text:
name: PrivacyPrinciple
annotations:
  iso29100_clause:
    tag: iso29100_clause
    value: '6'
  principle_count:
    tag: principle_count
    value: '11'
description: The eleven privacy principles defined in ISO/IEC 29100:2024 Clause 6
  that govern the processing of PII in ICT systems.
in_subset:
- privacy_principles
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/iso29100
rank: 1000
permissible_values:
  consent_and_choice:
    text: consent_and_choice
    description: 'Schema value for privacy principle 1 (Clause 6.2): the organization
      gives PII principals meaningful choice over processing of their PII, manages
      opt-in consent mechanisms, and provides readily accessible means to withdraw
      consent.'
    meaning: dpv:InformedConsent
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '6.2'
      principle_number:
        tag: principle_number
        value: '1'
  purpose_legitimacy_and_specification:
    text: purpose_legitimacy_and_specification
    description: 'Schema value for privacy principle 2 (Clause 6.3): the organization
      ensures each processing purpose is lawful, relies on a valid legal basis, and
      is communicated to PII principals prior to or at the time of collection.'
    meaning: dpv:Purpose
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '6.3'
      principle_number:
        tag: principle_number
        value: '2'
  collection_limitation:
    text: collection_limitation
    description: 'Schema value for privacy principle 3 (Clause 6.4): the organization
      limits PII collection to what is lawful and strictly necessary to fulfil the
      stated processing purposes.'
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '6.4'
      principle_number:
        tag: principle_number
        value: '3'
  data_minimization:
    text: data_minimization
    description: 'Schema value for privacy principle 4 (Clause 6.5): the organization
      designs processing procedures and ICT systems to minimize the volume of PII
      handled and the number of parties with access to it.'
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '6.5'
      principle_number:
        tag: principle_number
        value: '4'
  use_retention_and_disclosure_limitation:
    text: use_retention_and_disclosure_limitation
    description: 'Schema value for privacy principle 5 (Clause 6.6): the organization
      limits use, retention, and disclosure of PII to the minimum needed for legitimate
      purposes, and securely disposes of PII once those purposes have been fulfilled.'
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '6.6'
      principle_number:
        tag: principle_number
        value: '5'
  accuracy_and_quality:
    text: accuracy_and_quality
    description: 'Schema value for privacy principle 6 (Clause 6.7): the organization
      ensures PII is accurate, complete, current, and fit for purpose, and establishes
      procedures for verification, correction, and ongoing quality control of processed
      PII.'
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '6.7'
      principle_number:
        tag: principle_number
        value: '6'
  openness_transparency_and_notice:
    text: openness_transparency_and_notice
    description: 'Schema value for privacy principle 7 (Clause 6.8): the organization
      provides PII principals with clear, accessible information about its PII processing
      policies and practices, and notifies them of material changes to those policies.'
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '6.8'
      principle_number:
        tag: principle_number
        value: '7'
  individual_participation_and_access:
    text: individual_participation_and_access
    description: 'Schema value for privacy principle 8 (Clause 6.9): the organization
      gives PII principals the ability to access and review their PII and to challenge
      its accuracy and completeness, subject to applicable law.'
    meaning: dpv:DataSubjectRight
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '6.9'
      principle_number:
        tag: principle_number
        value: '8'
  accountability:
    text: accountability
    description: 'Schema value for privacy principle 9 (Clause 6.10): the organization
      documents and communicates its privacy obligations, ensures equivalent protection
      when transferring PII to third parties, and maintains internal complaint-handling
      and redress procedures.'
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '6.10'
      principle_number:
        tag: principle_number
        value: '9'
  information_security:
    text: information_security
    description: 'Schema value for privacy principle 10 (Clause 6.11): the organization
      applies appropriate security controls across operational, functional, and strategic
      levels to protect PII against unauthorized access, loss, or misuse throughout
      its full lifecycle.'
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '6.11'
      principle_number:
        tag: principle_number
        value: '10'
  privacy_compliance:
    text: privacy_compliance
    description: 'Schema value for privacy principle 11 (Clause 6.12): the organization
      verifies and demonstrates compliance with data protection and privacy requirements
      through audits, independent internal controls, and ongoing privacy risk assessments.'
    meaning: dpv:ComplianceAssessment
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: '6.12'
      principle_number:
        tag: principle_number
        value: '11'

docs.title: Class: PrivacyPrincipleImplementation
docs.text:
Documentation of how a specific privacy principle from Clause 6 has been implemented within the organization's privacy framework, including measures taken and evidence of adherence.

URI: iso29100:PrivacyPrincipleImplementation

 classDiagram
    class PrivacyPrincipleImplementation
    click PrivacyPrincipleImplementation href "../PrivacyPrincipleImplementation/"
      NamedEntity <|-- PrivacyPrincipleImplementation
        click NamedEntity href "../NamedEntity/"

      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : applicable_controls_ref





        PrivacyPrincipleImplementation --> "*" PrivacyControl : applicable_controls_ref
        click PrivacyControl href "../PrivacyControl/"



      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : created_date

      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : description

      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : evidence_of_adherence

      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : gaps_identified

      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : id

      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : implementation_measures

      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : improvement_actions

      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : last_assessed_date

      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : modified_date

      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : name

      PrivacyPrincipleImplementation : principle





        PrivacyPrincipleImplementation --> "1" PrivacyPrinciple : principle
        click PrivacyPrinciple href "../PrivacyPrinciple/"

docs.title: Class: PrivacyStakeholder
docs.text:
Schema base class for the four actor types recognized by the privacy framework: PII principals, PII controllers, PII processors, and third parties. Provides shared contact and jurisdictional attributes common to all stakeholder roles.

  • NOTE: this is an abstract class and should not be instantiated directly

name: PrivacyStakeholder
annotations:
  iso29100_clause:
    tag: iso29100_clause
    value: '3.20'
description: 'Schema base class for the four actor types recognized by the privacy
  framework: PII principals, PII controllers, PII processors, and third parties. Provides
  shared contact and jurisdictional attributes common to all stakeholder roles.'
comments:
- 'Reference: ISO/IEC 29100:2024 Clause 3.20. ISO/IEC standards text is copyright
  ISO - not reproduced here.'
in_subset:
- privacy_framework_core
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/iso29100

related_mappings:
- iso27001:InterestedParty
is_a: NamedEntity
abstract: true
slots:
- contact_information
- jurisdictions

docs.title: Enum: SafeguardingRequirementFactor
docs.text:
Categories of factors that influence an organization's privacy safeguarding requirements, as described in Clause 5.5 and Figure 1.

URI: iso29100:SafeguardingRequirementFactor

docs.title: Permissible Values
docs.text: Value Meaning Description legal_and_regulatory dpv:LegalBasis Safeguarding requirements arising from applicable laws (international, nation... contractual dpv:Contract Safeguarding requirements set out in data processing agreements, company poli... business None Safeguarding requirements driven by the application's characteristics, indust... other None Safeguarding requirements arising from PII principal privacy preferences, int...

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Name Description requirement_factor Category of factor driving this safeguarding requirement

docs.title: In Subsets
docs.text: • PrivacySafeguarding

docs.title: Annotations
docs.text: property value iso29100_clause 5.5

docs.title: Schema Source
docs.text: • from schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/iso29100

docs.title: LinkML Source
docs.text:
name: SafeguardingRequirementFactor
annotations:
  iso29100_clause:
    tag: iso29100_clause
    value: '5.5'
description: Categories of factors that influence an organization's privacy safeguarding
  requirements, as described in Clause 5.5 and Figure 1.
in_subset:
- privacy_safeguarding
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/iso29100
rank: 1000
permissible_values:
  legal_and_regulatory:
    text: legal_and_regulatory
    description: Safeguarding requirements arising from applicable laws (international,
      national, and local), regulations, judicial decisions, and negotiated labour
      or work-council agreements.
    meaning: dpv:LegalBasis
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: 5.5.2
  contractual:
    text: contractual
    description: Safeguarding requirements set out in data processing agreements,
      company policies, and binding corporate rules agreed between PII controllers,
      processors, and other parties.
    meaning: dpv:Contract
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: 5.5.3
  business:
    text: business
    description: Safeguarding requirements driven by the application's characteristics,
      industry guidelines, codes of conduct, or standards voluntarily adopted by the
      organization as part of its business model.
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: 5.5.4
  other:
    text: other
    description: Safeguarding requirements arising from PII principal privacy preferences,
      internal control frameworks, or technical standards voluntarily adopted by the
      organization.
    annotations:
      iso29100_clause:
        tag: iso29100_clause
        value: 5.5.5

docs.title: Type: Sparqlpath
docs.text:
A string encoding a SPARQL Property Path. The value of the string MUST conform to SPARQL syntax and SHOULD dereference to zero or more valid objects within the current instance document when encoded as RDF.

docs.title: Class: ThirdParty
docs.text:
Schema class for an external entity that receives PII outside the PII processor relationship — i.e., as a recipient that typically becomes a PII controller in its own right. Captures the receiving purpose and the legal basis for the transfer.

URI: iso29100:ThirdParty

 classDiagram
    class ThirdParty
    click ThirdParty href "../ThirdParty/"
      PrivacyStakeholder <|-- ThirdParty
        click PrivacyStakeholder href "../PrivacyStakeholder/"

      ThirdParty : contact_information

      ThirdParty : created_date

      ThirdParty : description

      ThirdParty : id

      ThirdParty : jurisdictions

      ThirdParty : modified_date

      ThirdParty : name

      ThirdParty : receiving_purpose

      ThirdParty : transfer_basis

docs.title: Inheritance
docs.text:
• NamedEntity
    • PrivacyStakeholder
      • ThirdParty

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Name Cardinality and Range Description Inheritance receiving_purpose 0..1  String Purpose for which the third party receives PII direct transfer_basis 0..1  String Legal or contractual basis for transferring PII to this third party direct contact_information 0..1  String Contact details for the privacy stakeholder PrivacyStakeholder jurisdictions *  String Jurisdictions in which the privacy stakeholder operates PrivacyStakeholder id 1  Uriorcurie Unique identifier for this entity instance NamedEntity name 1  String Human-readable name or title NamedEntity description 0..1  String Detailed description of the entity NamedEntity created_date 0..1  Date Date when the entity record was created NamedEntity modified_date 0..1  Date Date when the entity record was last modified NamedEntity

docs.title: Usages
docs.text: used by used in type used PrivacyFramework third_parties range ThirdParty

docs.title: In Subsets
docs.text: • PrivacyFrameworkCore

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
• Third parties do not process PII on behalf of the original PII controller
  • Upon receiving PII, a third party typically assumes controller responsibilities
  • Reference: ISO/IEC 29100:2024 Clause 3.24 and 5.2.5. ISO/IEC standards text is copyright ISO - not reproduced here.

docs.title: Annotations
docs.text: property value iso29100_clause 3.24

docs.title: Type: Time
docs.text:
A time object represents a (local) time of day, independent of any particular day

URI: xsd:time

docs.title: iso29100
docs.text:
A comprehensive LinkML schema modeling the ISO/IEC 29100:2024 privacy framework for information and communication technology (ICT) systems. Captures actors and roles, PII recognition, privacy safeguarding requirements, the eleven privacy principles, and the privacy risk management process. This schema covers: - Privacy framework actors (PII principal, controller, processor, third party) - Personally identifiable information (PII) and its categories - Privacy principles (11 principles from Clause 6) - Privacy safeguarding requirements and influencing factors - Privacy risk management including privacy impact assessment - Privacy policies, privacy controls, consent records, and privacy preferences

URI: https://w3id.org/lmodel/iso29100
